body:
https://github.com/xlab2016/space_db_private/pull/20#issuecomment-4104547747 - contains telegram bot token `8490528355:AAGHeNpjZJqWEytzt4iw1kfW2ouXlyItHCY` (it is now revoked).

We need to find the root cause of why it was leaked, and find a way to harden security of our systems, so every comment that our bot does publish automatically into repository is stripped away from our local tokens:

gh token, claude tokens, codex tokens, opencode tokens, agent cli tokens, gemini tokens, qwen tokens, telegram token.

So no token should ever be printed on screen, or log, or published as comment, as code and so on (should be covered with ***, and keep only first 3 and last 3 characters for purpose of comparison and debug).

If any token was leaked, we need immediate warning in telegram of the owner of chat, where the task was executed.

We cannot fully control AI, but we can check changes in pull requests for tokens, and monitor all comments, pull request description, issue comments, issue description and so on. We should check for active tokens.

We also should be able to list all our active tokens for debug purposes in private messages of owner of the chat with /tokens command, that command should not be documented, and work only in private messages for the owners of allowed chats.

That way I will be able to search on GitHub for other leaked tokens.

We need to download all logs and data related about the issue to this repository, make sure we compile that data to `./docs/case-studies/issue-{id}` folder, and use it to do deep case study analysis (also make sure to search online for additional facts and data), in which we will reconstruct timeline/sequence of events, find root causes of the problem, and propose possible solutions (including known existing components/libraries, that solve similar problem or can help in solutions).

If there is not enough data to find actual root cause, add debug output and verbose mode if not present, that will allow us to find root cause on next iteration.

If issue related to any other repository/project, where we can report issues on GitHub, please do so. Each issue must contain reproducible examples, workarounds and suggestions for fix the issue in code.

Please plan and execute everything in a single pull request, you have unlimited time and context, as context autocompacts and you can continue indefinetely, until it is each and every requirement fully addressed, and everything is totally done.
title: Telegram bot token was leaked
